J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the codebase never caught up... GSC shows two separate problems... Every row in gsc/Queries.csv is an InboxZap-era term (email cleanup, gmail storage, unsubscribe, inbox zero)
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot across all three surfaces (live/built product = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = stale) and frames every recommendation around it, exactly matching anchor_10.

J2.basis: Old /blog/post-1…post-12 URLs (indexed under email-cleanup topics that no longer exist) now 301 to /blog instead of 404ing
J2.rationale: Gives the 12 legacy posts an explicit disposition (301 to /blog) and cites aggregate GSC numbers (~566 impressions/mo, ~18 clicks/mo on brand terms), but applies one blanket redirect rather than per-asset/tiered decisions grounded in each post's actual impressions/positions, so it falls short of the asset-by-asset anchor_10.

J3.basis: captures the ~18 clicks/mo... still landing on old brand queries... instead of losing that residual equity... build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine
J3.rationale: Articulates the tension and lands a defensible middle path — harvest residual brand equity via the inboxzap-is-now-lumina bridge and 301s while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — avoiding both failure modes; just short of a fuller sequenced transition plan for full marks.

J1.basis: "Lumina pivoted from \"InboxZap\" (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the codebase never caught up" and config notes: "All historical GSC data ... reflects the old InboxZap positioning - zero historical visibility for the new product category"
J1.rationale: Nails the pivot: identifies live product = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = stale, and frames every recommendation around that reality (config, clusters, and report all restate it).

J2.basis: "Old /blog/post-1…post-12 URLs (indexed under email-cleanup topics that no longer exist) now 301 to /blog instead of 404ing" via a single `source: '/blog/post-:id(\\d+)'` redirect
J2.rationale: Gives the 12 legacy posts a real disposition (blanket 301 to /blog rather than 404, plus harvesting residual brand equity via the inboxzap-is-now-lumina post) but applies one uniform rule instead of walking them asset-by-asset with per-post impressions/positions — it only cites aggregate brand-query numbers, not each post's data.

J3.basis: "GSC shows two separate problems, not one" — captures the ~18 clicks/mo brand equity via redirects + brand-transition post, while sequencing the meeting-assistant cluster (comparison pages → platform gaps → TOFU pillar) as the growth engine
J3.rationale: Lands a defensible middle path: preserves/redirects salvageable legacy equity rather than torching it, doesn't double down on dead inbox-cleanup content, and explicitly sequences the transition with a prioritized backlog for the new positioning.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the codebase never caught up... Every row in gsc/Queries.csv is an InboxZap-era term... the pivot happened but the content strategy never followed
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly frames GSC data as legacy InboxZap demand and the repo as stale, and reframes every recommendation around the meeting-assistant reality. Textbook anchor_10.

J2.basis: Old /blog/post-1…post-12 URLs (indexed under email-cleanup topics that no longer exist) now 301 to /blog instead of 404ing
J2.rationale: There is a disposition and it's data-aware (brand queries harvested into inboxzap-is-now-lumina with real ~566 impressions/~18 clicks), but the 12 legacy posts get one blanket 301-to-/blog rather than an asset-by-asset walk citing each post's impressions/positions; no individual harvest-vs-sunset judgments.

J3.basis: GSC shows two separate problems, not one... captures the ~18 clicks/mo... instead of losing that residual equity... Priority backlog: comparison/alternative pages... build the meeting-assistant cluster
J3.rationale: Explicitly holds the tension: preserves salvageable brand equity via redirect + transition post while making the meeting-assistant cluster the growth engine and sequencing the sprints. Avoids both failure modes cleanly.
